member.comment: joint form for Senators Kelly and Gallego
member.office_address: 516 Hart Senate Office Building Washington DC 20510

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: This request form is a joint request form for the Offices of Senators Kelly and Gallego. This form must be completed for your appropriations request to be considered. Please keep in mind that you cannot save your answers to the Microsoft Form and come back to it later. Be prepared to submit it in one sitting.

sections.description: Please provide information about the person and organization submitting this request.

fields.label: Full name
Title
Email
Direct Phone Number
Government Affairs Firm
fields.help_text: If applicable, provide the name of the firm representing your organization.

fields.label: Organization Name
Street Address
City
County
Zip Code

sections.description: Select the subcommittee responsible for the funding area of your request.

fields.label: Please select the relevant Appropriations subcommittee for your request:
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services,�Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA is funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee as per standard appropriations structure and the provided reference values.

sections.description: Provide the technical details of the program and your funding or language request.

fields.label: What is the program name or title of this request?
fields.rationale: Standard program name for this account as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Please select the type of request.
fields.rationale: The context specifies this as a programmatic funding request.

fields.label: Under which federal agency and/or office does this program fall?�
fields.rationale: The Science Mission Directorate is an office within NASA.

fields.label: Under which account(s) does this federal program fall?
fields.rationale: The SMD is funded through the 'Science' account within the NASA budget.

fields.label: Please indicate the funding level you are requesting for this federal program in FY27.
fields.rationale: This is the specific topline funding ask provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level for FY26.
fields.rationale: This reflects the FY2026 enacted level according to H.R. 6938.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level for FY24/FY25.
fields.rationale: Using the FY2025 enacted level as the most recent baseline.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level in the President's FY27 budget request.
fields.rationale: The President's FY2027 Budget Request has not been released yet.

fields.label: Please provide the BILL language you wish to include in the FY27 appropriations bill.
fields.rationale: The provided reference values state that bill language is not applicable for this request.

fields.label: Was this bill language included in the FY26 appropriations bill?
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate topline, not a specific bill language request.

fields.label: Please provide the REPORT language you wish to include in the FY27 appropriations bill.
fields.rationale: The provided reference values state that report language is not applicable for this request.

fields.label: Was this report language included in the FY26 appropriations bill?
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate topline, not a specific report language request.

sections.description: Additional technical fields for research and development or branched requests.

fields.label: What is the program name or title of this request?
fields.rationale: Duplicate field.

fields.label: Please select the type of request.
fields.rationale: Duplicate field.

fields.label: Under which federal agency and/or office does this program fall?�
fields.rationale: Duplicate field.

fields.label: Under which account(s) does this federal program fall?
fields.rationale: Duplicate field.

fields.label: Please provide the appropriate Program Element (PE) line number for R&D accounts.
fields.rationale: The request is for the SMD topline, which covers hundreds of PE lines; listing a single one would be inaccurate.

fields.label: Please indicate program funding level in the President's FY27 budget request.
fields.rationale: The President's FY2027 Budget Request has not been released yet.

fields.label: Please indicate the funding level you are requesting ABOVE the amount included (or not included) in the President's budget request.
fields.rationale: Cannot calculate the delta until the President's FY2027 request is public.

sections.description: Explain the significance of this request and its relevance to Arizona.

fields.label: Request Justification�
fields.draft_value:
* Restores NASA Science to a $9 billion trajectory, ensuring U.S. leadership remains unchallenged by China's rapidly expanding space science ambitions.
* Provides essential workforce stability for thousands of specialized contractors and civil servants following the disruptive budget proposals and uncertainty of FY 2026.
* Funds peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities that ensure the highest scientific return on investment and address the most critical questions in space and earth science.
* Drives economic growth and innovation across the aerospace industrial base, returning approximately $3 to the economy for every $1 invested.
fields.rationale: Justification draws from the $9B ask, the net increase over FY26, and the strategic talking points regarding China, workforce stability, and economic ROI.

fields.label: Arizona Connection
fields.help_text: Describe how this request impacts or relates to the state of Arizona.
fields.draft_value:
* Arizona received over $422 million in NASA Science obligations between FY 2023 and FY 2025, supporting a robust statewide aerospace and research economy.
* Directly supports the Northrop Grumman facility in Gilbert, which is currently executing a $569 million contract for the JPSS-2 spacecraft.
* Funds world-class research at the University of Arizona, including over $486 million in cumulative awards for James Webb Space Telescope instrumentation.
* Powers the Psyche metal asteroid mission at Arizona State University, exploring planetary core formation through an active $45 million award.
fields.rationale: References specific NASA spending data for Arizona, including the $422M obligation total and major active awards for Northrop Grumman, UA, and ASU.

fields.label: How many requests for different programs/ projects does your organization plan on submitting?
fields.rationale: This is user-specific information regarding the submitter's internal organizational plans.

fields.label: If your organization is submitting multiple requests for different programs/ projects, please indicate the priority of this request compared to other requests you have submitted.�
options: Select this option if you are only submitting a request for one program/ project.
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is specific to the submitter's internal organizational strategy and cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Please list any Members of the House of Representatives to whom you are submitting the same request.
fields.draft_value: Rep. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Rep. Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: These are the House leads for the NASA Science letter as identified in the reference context.

fields.label: Please provide any additional information that you would like our offices to know about your request.
fields.draft_value:
* Space science represents less than one-third of one percent of federal spending yet delivers transformative discoveries, national prestige, and practical applications like weather forecasting.
* The requested $9 billion level is necessary to maintain the scientific community's consensus roadmaps and prevent the loss of critical technical capabilities developed over decades.
* Public polling consistently shows space exploration is a non-partisan priority for the American people, receiving broad support for funding restoration after the FY 2026 proposed cuts.
fields.rationale: Utilizes secondary advocacy talking points regarding federal budget share, decadal survey importance, and public support levels.

fields.label: Do you plan send documentation to support this request to appropriations@kelly.senate.gov and appropriations@gallego.senate.gov using the subject line template above? As a reminder, this is not a requirement.
fields.rationale: This field requires knowledge of the submitter's future administrative actions.

top_contracts.description: Joint polar satellite system-2 spacecraft.  Gd-300hp core spacecraft including all associated hardware, software, source code, maintenance system and documentation for the jpss-2 spacecraft mission.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing the Joint Polar Satellite System-2 spacecraft.

top_contracts.description: Next generation Space Telescope nircam the contractor shall provide the personnel, services, materials, equipment, and facilities needed to manage and perform phases a through E of the selected near-infrared camera (NIRCAM) instrument flight investigation.  The contractor is responsible for ensuring that all the requirements in the contract are met.  The contractor shall perform the following activities within each phase: phase a:  the contractor shall define the nircam science investigation, science payload, and Mission Operations and Data systems. The contractor shall document its findings and conclusions in a concept study report that it shall prepare and deliver to the government.  The contractor shall define and refine its Education and Public Outreach plan during phase a. Phases b-d:  the contractor shall design, analyze, develop, fabricate, calibrate, integrate, test and deliver the nircam science instrument to National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) goddard Space flight center'S (GSFC) James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) project. The contractor shall also plan and prepare for the Mission Operations phase during phases b-d. This shall include developing an Operations and calibration strategy, defining and planning the Guaranteed Time Observations (GTO) science program, preparing and testing ground software in collaboration with the science and Operations center, and participating in Mission simulations.  The contractor shall execute its Education and Public Outreach (E/PO) plan during phases b-d. Phases E:  the contractor shall provide the necessary personnel, services, materials, equipment, and facilities needed to perform Mission Operations and Data Analysis (MO&DA) activities.  The contractor shall direct the execution of its gto science program during phase E.  The contractor shall continue to execute its E/po plan during phase E.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing the critical near-infrared camera for the JWST mission.

top_contracts.description: Osiris - rex asteroid sample return mission. 1.1 osiris - rex deputy principal investigator, heather enos, osiris - rex business manager, Dr. Drake and Dr. Lauretta will have the overall responsibility for the development and delivery of the osiris - rex phase a Concept Study Report. The Concept Study Report must meet the requirements delineated in part ii of the statement of work. 1.2 the Concept Study Report (CSR) will include, but not limited to, proposed level 1 science requirements, criteria for full mission success satisfying the baseline science mission and criteria for minimum mission success satisfying the threshold science mission. 1.3 the ua management team will work closely with the GSFC and lm management teams to refine the proposed osiris - rex mission costs. The ua management team will work closely with GSFC project management and lm to assess the robustness and feasibility of the cost and schedules. The ua management team will have the lead for the cost section of the Report. 1.4 the ua management team will provide support in the development of required international agreements such as technical assistance agreements and itar licenses. 1.5 the ua management team will oversee the phase a contract with instrument lead, Dr. Phil christensen, asu. 1.6 the ua management team will provide inputs and validation support to the GSFC integrated master scheduler. 1.7 deputy PI, Dr. Lauretta, will lead the development of the E/po and student collaboration plans. 1.8 the University of Arizona (UA) will support the OSIRIS-REx site visit at lockheed martin, littleton, co.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing the concept study for the OSIRIS-REx asteroid sample return.

top_contracts.description: Igf::CL,ct::igf Psyche: journey to a metal world  this contract provides for work to be performed by the Principal Investigator in support of the phase a concept definition portion of the NASA discovery program 2014 Announcement of Opportunity (AO) (nnh14zda014o) solicitation dated november 5, 2014.  The mission Principal Investigator (PI), Dr. Linda elkins-tanton, of the school of Earth and space exploration at Arizona State University (ASU), is responsible for the success and scientific integrity of the mission.  As such, the PI shall lead the project, manage project resources, and direct all activities of the science team carried out in support of the mission.  Specifically, during phase a, the PI will lead the mission team in conducting the concept study report and associated site visit.  Should the mission be selected to continue into phase b, the PI will lead the team through the initiation of the phase b effort with a transition phase between notification of award and establishment of the phase b contract.   The PI is assisted by a deputy PI, Dr. James bell at Arizona State University.  Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is the mission major partner for spacecraft development and mission implementation, and shall support the PI with a project team experienced in management, systems engineering, mission design, payload development, safety and mission assurance, navigation, and mission operations.  Specific objectives to address the phase a criteria and guidelines are:  - project management for the phase a contract - conduct and support of phase a project reviews - phase b/c/d/E spacecraft development and mission planning - phase b/c/d/E cost estimates - concept study report preparation - orals/site visit support by key personnel  this contract provides for work to be performed by the Principal Investigator in support of the phase a concept definition portion of the NASA discovery program 2014 Announcement of Opportunity (AO) (nnh14zda014o) solicitation dated november 5, 2014.  The mission Principal Investigator (PI), Dr. Linda elkins-tanton, of the school of Earth and space exploration at Arizona State University (ASU), is responsible for the success and scientific integrity of the mission.  As such, the PI shall lead the project, manage project resources, and direct all activities of the science team carried out in support of the mission.  Specifically, during phase a, the PI will lead the mission team in conducting the concept study report and associated site visit.  Should the mission be selected to continue into phase b, the PI will lead the team through the initiation of the phase b effort with a transition phase between notification of award and establishment of the phase b contract.   The PI is assisted by a deputy PI, Dr. James bell at Arizona State University.  Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is the mission major partner for spacecraft development and mission implementation, and shall support the PI with a project team experienced in management, systems engineering, mission design, payload development, safety and mission assurance, navigation, and mission operations.  Specific objectives to address the phase a criteria and guidelines are:  - project management for the phase a contract - conduct and support of phase a project reviews - phase b/c/d/E spacecraft development and mission planning - phase b/c/d/E cost estimates - concept study report preparation - orals/site visit support by key personnel
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Exploring planetary core formation via the Psyche metal asteroid mission.

top_contracts.description: The contractor shall provide the necessary personnel, facilities, services, and materials to design, code, integrate and test the OSIRIS-REx flight dynamics system to support the OSIRIS-REx launch and flight operations to retrieve a sample of the neo and return the sample to Earth. After launch, kinetx shall provide operations support for 30 days. The scope of this SOW covers phase c/d of the OSIRIS-REx life cycle. This work shall be performed in accordance with the requirements of this document and all attachments to the contract.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing flight dynamics for OSIRIS-REx asteroid sample return.
Transitioning commercial rapid-response satellite technology to NASA use.

top_contracts.description: Lucy flight dynamics systems navigational phase b/c/d/E
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Engineering navigation systems for the Lucy asteroid mission.

top_grants.description: All components of this design are trl 6 or higher and require no technology development  making this in line with the scope of the astrophysics pioneers call.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Advancing a high-readiness astrophysics instrument design.

top_grants.description: With the discovery that habitable planets may be very common  one of the most compelling and timely questions to answer is: which nearby planetary systems are likely to host habitable planets and life? Advancing our knowledge on this question will
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Searching nearby planetary systems for signs of life.

top_grants.description: 24-pimove24-0003  odin - optomechanical-distributed instrument for inertial sensing and navigation
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing an advanced instrument for precise space navigation.

top_grants.description: Engaging hispanic communities in authentic NASA science: broadening participation in science activation through local partnerships and national networks (engaging hispanic communities)
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Broadening NASA science participation through Hispanic community partnerships.

top_grants.description: 23-yorpd23_2-0018  the catalina sky survey for near-earth objects
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Tracking near-Earth objects via the Catalina Sky Survey.
